meaning: adj.同形的,同型性的(指器官或有机体)的
examples: "Homomorphic encryption gives you a way out of the dilemma," Gentry says.
“Homomorphic encryption gives you a way out of the dilemma,” Gentry says.
The homomorphic nature of the XGrind compressed format grants XGrind many interesting features
Things like measuring and reducing attack surface, self-defending data, damage-potential effort ratios, fully homomorphic encryption, and end-to-end message confidentiality.
CryptDB works by turning data into "homomorphic" information: strings of numbers, which you can then calculate against one another to get the answers you require.
